description:
Create electronics the easy way.

LibrePCB is a free, cross-platform, easy-to-use electronic design automation suite to draw schematics and design printed circuit boards – for makers, students and professionals, from beginners to experts.
This software just lets you develop electronics the right way. No costs. No restrictions. No online account. No unnecessary complexity.
Today we released LibrePCB 2.1.1, which includes some improvements related to FreeRouting compatibility and keyboard shortcuts on macOS, and also various small bugfixes. Check out the changelog below.

Changelog

Schematic & Board Editor:
- Fix accidentally selecting objects on hidden layers (#1836)
- Fix rejecting deletion of duplicate assembly options (#1831)

Import/Export:
- DSN Export: Always include a pad stack for the default via preset (#1844)
- SES Import: Fix incompatibility with recent FreeRouting versions (#1842)

User Interface:
- Show native modifier key text on macOS (#1846, #1848)
- Accept locale-dependent decimal point in attribute values (#1839)
- Fix error message after aborting opening a library (78e26700)
- Fix outdated instructions in background image dialog (#1845)
- Fix missing top border in about panel (627cea08)

Miscellaneous:
- Render attribute values in schematics & boards independent of locale (#1838)
- Fix project migration log not opening in Snap/Flatpak (#1815)
- Fix crash when canceling attributes overlay in device editor (#1840)

Credits
External code contributors of this release: @SiboVG

Download
The release can be downloaded for all major operating systems from our download page.
If you like LibrePCB, please consider making a small donation to support the ongoing development.
Thank you!
